history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' after Spring Boot 3 upgrade because the javax.mail dependency was removed and replaced with jakarta.mail, but the import migration or dependency declaration was incomplete.
history.qwen_rationale: Inserting a pom_patch step to explicitly add jakarta.mail:jakarta.mail-api and org.eclipse.angus:angus-mail ensures the Jakarta Mail API is on the classpath before the Spring Boot 3 upgrade runs. Adding JavaxMigrationToJakarta explicitly to the upgrade step guarantees the javax.mail imports are rewritten to jakarta.mail, resolving the missing class error.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed because MailService.java still references javax.mail.MessagingException after the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, indicating the JavaxMigrationToJakarta recipe did not successfully rewrite the javax.mail imports/types when bundled in the same step.
history.qwen_rationale: Splitting the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade and the Jakarta namespace migration into separate steps ensures the POM is fully updated to Spring Boot 3.3 (which provides jakarta.mail-api) before the JavaxMigrationToJakarta recipe runs. This gives the recipe a clean classpath to resolve the new jakarta.mail types and reliably rewrite all javax.mail references in the source code.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails at upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 because Spring Boot 3.3 dependencies switched to jakarta.mail but source code still imports javax.mail, causing a class file not found error before the Jakarta migration recipe could run.
history.qwen_rationale: By explicitly including JavaxMigrationToJakarta in the same step as UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3, the source code will be migrated to jakarta namespaces before the post-step compilation occurs, resolving the missing class error and aligning the codebase with the new Spring Boot 3.x classpath.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' because Spring Boot 3 upgrade removes javax.mail dependencies and the JavaxMigrationToJakarta recipe did not rewrite the imports, likely due to missing jakarta.mail on the classpath during recipe execution.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding jakarta.mail:jakarta.mail-api via pom_patch before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures the Jakarta mail API is on the classpath when JavaxMigrationToJakarta runs, enabling it to correctly map javax.mail to jakarta.mail. Separating the Jakarta migration into its own step under JDK 11 guarantees it executes before the SB upgrade alters the POM, preventing classpath resolution delays that caused the previous failure.

history.qwen_observation: Manual JavaxMigrationToJakarta step changed imports to jakarta.* but Spring Boot 2.7.3 still provided javax.* dependencies, causing missing package and type mismatch errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Remove the manual jakarta migration and mail dependency steps. Let UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 handle the javax-to-jakarta migration and dependency updates internally, as it is designed to compose these changes safely when upgrading from SB 2.x to 3.x.
